perturbation.original_text: Each party agrees that the other may disclose Proprietary Information received by it to its Affiliates, employees not permitted under the Credit Agreement and agents, subject to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party agrees that the other may disclose Proprietary Information received by it to its Affiliates, employees and agents, without needing to adhere to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "subject to the terms of this Agreement", this perturbation now contradicts Section 5 ("Duty of Care") and its requirement that all Proprietary Information must be retained by the Recipient in accordance with its customary procedures for handling confidential information. The recipient is no longer bound by the same duty of care, creating a contradiction regarding how disclosed information should be protected by affiliates, employees, and agents.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All Proprietary Information must be retained by the Recipient in accordance with its customary procedures for handling confidential information of this nature and disclosed only to the Recipient's Affiliates or employees (or , attorneys, accountants and agents who have a non-disclosure obligation at least as restrictive as this Agreement) who need to know such information for purposes of the servicing or protection of its interest in respect of the Loans, the Credit Agreement and the Loan Documents (each as defined under the Credit Agreement) and the transactions contemplated thereby and to such third parties as the Discloser has consented to by prior written approval. In addition, the Recipient must provide the same care to avoid disclosure not permitted under the Credit Agreement or unauthorized use of the Proprietary Information as it provides to protect its own similar proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'subject to the terms of this Agreement', which directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text ensures that any disclosure of proprietary information to affiliates, employees, and agents remains bound by the agreement's terms, particularly regarding the duty of care. By removing this constraint, the changed text allows disclosure without adherence to the same duty of care outlined in the contradicted section, creating a direct conflict and weakening the protection of proprietary information.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, all copies of written, recorded, graphical or other tangible Proprietary Information must either be returned to the Discloser, or destroyed (i) after the Recipient's need for it has expired or (ii) upon the request of the Discloser.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this Agreement, all copies of written, recorded, graphical or other tangible Proprietary Information must be retained by the Recipient, regardless of the Discloser's request or the Recipient's needs.
perturbation.explanation: The change directly contradicts the original requirement to return or destroy Proprietary Information upon termination. The text explicitly states that Proprietary Information must be retained, which directly clashes with the obligation to return the data or destroy it. The phrase "regardless of the Discloser's request or the Recipient's needs" removes any exceptions where the proprietary information can be discarded. Section 6 is directly violated in its entirety, as the Recipient cannot return or destroy said information
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership. All Proprietary Information, unless otherwise specified in writing, (a) remains the property of the Discloser, and (b) must be used by the Recipient only for the purpose stated herein. Upon termination of this Agreement, all copies of written, recorded, graphical or other tangible Proprietary Information must either be returned to the Discloser, or destroyed (i) after the Recipient's need for it has expired or (ii) upon the request of the Discloser. At the request of the Discloser, the Recipient will furnish a certificate of an officer of the Recipient certifying that any Proprietary Information not returned to Discloser has been destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text. The original and contradicted text both require the return or destruction of Proprietary Information upon termination of the agreement. The changed text mandates that the recipient retains this information, 'regardless of the Discloser's request or the Recipient's needs,' creating a clear and direct conflict with the obligation to return or destroy said information. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition provided.

perturbation.original_text: Each party warrants that it has the right to disclose all Proprietary Information which it will disclose to the other party pursuant to this Agreement, and each party agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the other from all claims by a third party related to the wrongful disclosure of such third party's information. Otherwise, neither party makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, with respect to any Proprietary Information. Neither party is liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages of any nature or kind resulting from or arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party warrants that it has the right to disclose all Proprietary Information which it will disclose to the other party pursuant to this Agreement. Otherwise, neither party makes any representation or warranty, express or implied, with respect to any Proprietary Information. Neither party is liable for indirect, incidental, consequential, or punitive damages of any nature or kind resulting from or arising in connection with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the phrase "and each party agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the other from all claims by a third party related to the wrongful disclosure of such third party's information." Thus, it removes the indemnification clause, meaning that if a party discloses 3rd party information, there is no protection between the parties in the contract. This contradicts section 8, because there is no longer a clear allocation of liability in the event of unauthorized disclosure. This will make it difficult to claim monetary damages. Now, section 8, which states, 'a Recipient's unauthorized disclosure or use of Proprietary Information may result in irreparable harm,' could be toothless, as there is no clearly defined indemnification clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that a Recipient's unauthorized disclosure or use of Proprietary Information may result in irreparable harm. Therefore, the parties agree that, in the event of violation or threatened violation of this Agreement, without limiting any other rights and remedies of each other, a temporary restraining order and/or an injunction to enjoin disclosure of Proprietary Information may be sought against the party who has breached or threatened to breach this Agreement and the party who has breached or threatened to breach this Agreement will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.justification: The original text included an indemnification clause, protecting parties from claims related to wrongful disclosure of third-party information. The changed text removes this clause. The contradicted text acknowledges that unauthorized disclosure of proprietary information may cause irreparable harm and allows for injunctions. By removing the indemnification clause, the changed text creates a contradiction because there is no longer a clear allocation of liability if a party wrongfully discloses information, potentially undermining the effectiveness of the injunctive relief mentioned in the contradicted text. This aligns with the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition as the removal of essential language creates a contradiction with the obligations stated in the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Failure by a party to enforce or exercise any provision, right or option contained in this Agreement will not be construed as a present or future waiver of such provision, right or option.
perturbation.changed_text: Failure by a party to enforce or exercise any provision, right or option contained in this Agreement will be construed as a present waiver of such provision, right or option.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the original 'no waiver' clause. Now, failure to enforce a right is considered a waiver of that right. This creates a contradiction with Section 8, which discusses the 'Right to Enjoin Disclosure.' If the Discloser does not immediately seek an injunction upon the first instance of threatened disclosure, this modified clause suggests they have waived their right to seek an injunction later, even if the harm becomes more severe. This undermines the ability to protect Proprietary Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The parties acknowledge that a Recipient's unauthorized disclosure or use of Proprietary Information may result in irreparable harm. Therefore, the parties agree that, in the event of violation or threatened violation of this Agreement, without limiting any other rights and remedies of each other, a temporary restraining order and/or an injunction to enjoin disclosure of Proprietary Information may be sought against the party who has breached or threatened to breach this Agreement and the party who has breached or threatened to breach this Agreement will not raise the defense of an adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that failure to enforce a right is now considered a waiver, directly conflicting with the contradicted text, which grants the right to seek an injunction for unauthorized disclosure. The change undermines the right to seek an injunction later, creating a strong in-text contradiction as described in the Perturbation Explanation.
